name: ship-a-feature
description: Default end-to-end feature shape: orient, plan, coordinate, slice, test, review, land, account. Each stage is a literal maddu invocation so walking it exercises the real cockpit surfaces (Plans, Reviews, Pipelines, Loops, Cost).

stages.name: orient
stages.intent: Read the situation and register. `maddu status` to read active state, `maddu brief` for the situation brief, then `maddu session register --runtime claude-code --role implementer --label "<feature>" --focus "<one-line goal>"`.

stages.name: plan
stages.intent: Set the goal and open a plan. `maddu goal set "<feature goal>"`, then `maddu plan new "<feature>"` and `maddu plan add-phase <plan-id> "<phase>"` for each phase. Surfaces in the cockpit Plans route.

stages.name: coordinate
stages.intent: Hand the plan to a coordinator for multi-lane work. `maddu coordinator <plan-id>` to drive phase execution; the coordinator claims lanes and dispatches workers.

stages.name: slice
stages.intent: Declare the working slice scope before editing. `maddu slice scope-declare --paths "<dirs>" --focus "<what this slice changes>"` so lane ownership and audit trail are explicit.

stages.name: test
stages.intent: Verify the change. Run the project test suite, then `maddu test` and `maddu doctor` to surface FAIL rows and hard-rule violations.

stages.name: review
stages.intent: Open a structured review of the slice-stop. `maddu review run --slice <slice-event-id>` records the review against the slice event; surfaces in the cockpit Reviews route.

stages.name: land
stages.intent: Complete the phase and stop the slice. `maddu plan complete-phase <plan-id>` (completes the next open phase; pass a phase number to target one), then `maddu slice-stop "SLICE STOP: <summary>..."` with targets, paths, gates, and learnings.

stages.name: account
stages.intent: Account for the work. `maddu cost` to record spend against the run, then `maddu skill from-slice <slice-event-id>` to extract a reusable recipe from the slice-stop summary.
